issues.title: No user-visible data export / backup strategy
issues.description: All data is stored locally in the browser with no cloud sync. If the user clears browser data, moves devices, or the browser profile is lost/corrupted, there is no documented way to export or recover data.
issues.recommendation: Provide explicit import/export (JSON) and backup capabilities in the MVP: a user-initiated 'Export data' (download .json) and 'Import data' flow, as well as an optional periodic local snapshot (IndexedDB store or downloadable copy). Document data permanence and caveats prominently in the UI and README.

issues.title: IndexedDB availability and failure modes are not fully handled
issues.description: Browsers and modes (e.g., Safari private browsing) may disallow or throw on IndexedDB, and quota errors can occur. The spec mentions fallback to localStorage but does not define detection, migration, or user-visible handling of failures or QUOTA_EXCEEDED errors.
issues.recommendation: Implement robust feature detection and graceful fallback: on openDB failure detect reason, fall back to localStorage or in-memory store with clear UI/error message. Handle QUOTA errors with retries and user feedback. Centralize storage access error handling and surface meaningful messages and remediation steps (e.g., free space, switch browser). Test these failure paths in CI/E2E.

issues.title: No DB schema migration/versioning strategy
issues.description: Changing the DB schema (object stores, indexes, fields, keyPaths) needs well-defined upgrade handlers. The spec mentions version 1 but doesn't prescribe upgrade or data migration patterns for future versions.
issues.recommendation: Define and implement explicit migration routines tied to IndexedDB version upgrades (use idb's upgrade callback). Maintain a migration plan and unit tests for each schema change. Add validation and recovery steps (e.g., export current DB before migration, rollback/error reporting if migration fails).

issues.title: No cross-tab synchronization mechanism
issues.description: If a user opens multiple tabs/windows, changes in one tab will not automatically update other tabs. The proposed in-app EventEmitter won't propagate across browsing contexts, causing stale UI or conflicts.
issues.recommendation: Use BroadcastChannel (or storage events as fallback) to broadcast storage changes across tabs. On receiving events, invalidate caches or refresh relevant state. Ensure cross-tab messages are idempotent and include timestamps/operation ids for ordering.

issues.title: Unspecified behavior and atomicity when deleting projects
issues.description: Deleting a project can orphan tasks. The spec only briefly mentions cross-store transactions for future features without specifying cascade, reassign, or confirmation behavior.
issues.recommendation: Define and implement a clear UX and transactional behavior: either cascade-delete tasks, reassign tasks to 'no project', or require explicit per-task action. Implement the deletion as an IndexedDB transaction across object stores to keep data consistent and surface a confirmation dialog explaining consequences.

issues.title: Potential performance problems using React Context for global task state
issues.description: React Context can cause broad re-renders if global state changes frequently. With up to ~5k tasks and common operations (create/update/toggle), naive context usage could degrade performance.
issues.recommendation: Prefer a lightweight store with selector-based subscriptions (e.g., Zustand) or split state so updates are localized. Use memoization and React list virtualization (react-window/react-virtualized) for long lists. Benchmark common flows and test responsiveness with ~5k synthetic tasks.

issues.title: Static assets are not ensured to be available offline (no service worker/PWA)
issues.description: Spec asserts offline data availability but loading static assets (HTML/CSS/JS) still requires network unless a service worker caches them. Users expecting full offline use may see the app fail to load if offline and not previously cached.
issues.recommendation: Add an optional service worker (Workbox or simple SW) / PWA manifest to cache build assets so the app can load fully offline after first visit. Keep SW simple to avoid complexity with updates and caching invalidation; follow standard cache-busting via content-hashed assets.

issues.title: Accessibility requirements are underspecified
issues.description: The spec states 'basic accessibility' but lacks measurable requirements (WCAG level, keyboard focus rules, ARIA roles, contrast checks). Vague requirements make acceptance and CI tests ambiguous.
issues.recommendation: Expand accessibility requirements into a concrete checklist: required ARIA roles for dialogs/modals, focus traps on editors, keyboard shortcuts, color contrast levels, and automated axe checks in CI. Include manual acceptance criteria and tests for screen reader navigation of primary flows.

issues.title: XSS / rendering of user-provided content not fully specified
issues.description: The spec says descriptions should be plain text and escaped, but does not mandate enforcement. If any rendering uses innerHTML or introduces future formatting, XSS vectors arise.
issues.recommendation: Enforce plain-text rendering by default (no innerHTML). If supporting simple formatting later, use a vetted sanitizer (DOMPurify) with a strict whitelist. Add CSP recommendations (no-inline script/style) and ensure build tooling avoids inline scripts to enable strong CSP headers from hosting.

issues.title: Cross-browser IndexedDB quirks and E2E test coverage
issues.description: IndexedDB behavior differs across browsers (Safari, older browsers). The testing strategy mentions fake-indexeddb for unit tests but does not require E2E coverage for browser-specific idb behavior.
issues.recommendation: Include Playwright/Cypress tests running at least on Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit (Safari) in CI or as periodic checks to catch platform-specific IndexedDB or storage quota issues. Add tests validating persistence across reloads and private-mode behaviors where feasible.

issues.title: Bundle size and dependency risk with recommended tech stack
issues.description: React + TypeScript + idb + Tailwind can be kept small but may exceed the <1 MB target if additional libraries are introduced (icons, UI kits, big test libs).
issues.recommendation: Enforce bundle-size checks in CI. Use Vite optimizations, tree-shaking, Tailwind purge (or JIT) and prefer tiny libraries. Consider replacing heavy icon sets with SVG sprites or on-demand loading. Monitor and document dependency rationale and size impact.

issues.title: Observability, monitoring and dependency security are not prescribed
issues.description: There is no mention of local error logging (for debugging), optional user opt-in telemetry, or an automated dependency vulnerability check in CI beyond a brief mention.
issues.recommendation: Add CI steps for 'npm audit' / Snyk or Dependabot alerts and an optional in-app developer log accessible to the user for troubleshooting. If telemetry is added later, make it opt-in and documented.
